generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance BuildingTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-enabled temperature sensor deployed in buildings for real-time environmental monitoring, integrated with BIM models for spatial context."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance BuildingOccupancySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Occupancy detection sensor (PIR/thermal) capturing real-time occupancy data for HVAC optimization and space utilization analytics."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BuildingOccupancyDataTransmitted
]

instance EnergyConsumptionMeter : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Smart energy meter measuring real-time electricity consumption across building systems, synchronized with BIM electrical infrastructure models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitoringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vibration/acoustic sensors embedded in structural elements for continuous structural health monitoring, integrated with FEA models."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Dedicated sensors monitoring HVAC system performance metrics (airflow, pressure, efficiency) with direct BIM integration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingTemperatureSensor
]

instance BuildingOccupancyDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingOccupancySensor
]

instance EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnergyConsumptionMeter
]

instance StructuralHealthDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthMonitoringSensor
]

instance HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACPerformanceSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMGeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static geometric representation of building infrastructure from BIM tools (IFC standard), serving as foundational spatial context for DT deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMIntegrationEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance BCSDeviceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model of Building Control Systems components (sensors, actuators, controllers) with semantic mappings to BIM elements."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DeviceModelingEnabler, ControlAutomationEnabler
]

instance ThermalPerformanceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based thermal model for HVAC system simulation, integrating CFD data with real-time sensor inputs."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance StructuralHealthModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Finite Element Analysis model for structural integrity monitoring, synchronized with vibration sensor data."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngineEnabler
]

instance EnergyConsumptionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic energy consumption model combining real-time meter data with occupancy patterns for optimization scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EnergyAnalyticsEnabler, OptimizationEngineEnabler
]

instance RealTimeBuildingStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time data stream combining sensor inputs, BIM attributes, and operational metrics for continuous DT synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BuildingTemperatureDataTransmitted, BuildingOccupancyDataTransmitted, EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, HVACPerformanceDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataIntegrationEnabler, VisualizationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series historical data repository for building performance metrics, enabling trend analysis and predictive scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted, StructuralHealthDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance BIMIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration framework using IFC standard to harmonize BIM geometric models with real-time operational data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, VisualizationService
]

instance DeviceModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-driven engineering toolset for automated generation of BCS device models from BIM-enriched specifications."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAutomationService
]

instance SimulationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics simulation platform (ANSYS Fluent/OpenFOAM) executing multi-physics simulations for design validation and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationAndOptimizationService
]

instance DataIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Event-driven data pipeline using Apache Kafka for real-time fusion of heterogeneous data sources (IoT, BIM, legacy systems)."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D/4D/5D visualization platform (NVIDIA Omniverse) with AR/VR integration for multi-stakeholder collaboration."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning framework (TensorFlow/PyTorch) for training predictive models on historical and real-time building performance data."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance ControlAutomationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control system integrating DT insights with physical actuators via OPC UA/MQTT protocols."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlAutomationService
]

instance EnergyAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Specialized analytics engine for energy consumption optimization, incorporating lifecycle cost estimation models."
    DTDFVocab:enables EnergyOptimizationService
]

instance OptimizationEngineEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Genetic algorithm-based optimization platform for space utilization and energy efficiency scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationAndOptimizationService
]

instance LifecycleManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "End-to-end digital continuity framework supporting as-built model synchronization and adaptive refurbishment planning."
    DTDFVocab:enables LifecycleManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous data ingestion and validation service from heterogeneous sources (IoT, BIM, legacy systems) with anomaly detection capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingStateInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Machine learning-driven forecasting service for failure prediction, energy optimization, and lifecycle cost estimation."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight, EnergyOptimizationInsight, RefurbishmentRecommendationInsight
]

instance SimulationAndOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Multi-physics simulation service for design validation, operational optimization, and retrofit strategy testing."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationValidationInsight, OptimizationScenarioInsight
]

instance ControlAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Closed-loop control system integrating DT insights with physical actuators for automated adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACAdjustmentAction, DemandResponseAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D/4D/5D visualization service with AR/VR integration for remote inspections and collaborative reviews."
    DTDFVocab:provides BuildingVisualizationInsight, ClashDetectionInsight
]

instance LifecycleManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "End-to-end digital continuity service from design to decommissioning with adaptive refurbishment planning."
    DTDFVocab:provides AsBuiltSynchronizationInsight, RefurbishmentPlanInsight
]

instance EnergyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Specialized service for energy consumption optimization through dynamic load balancing and predictive scenarios."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight, LoadBalancingAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance BuildingStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of building operational state including environmental conditions, equipment status, and occupancy patterns."
]

    base:desc "Automated alert generation for detected equipment failures or performance anomalies."

]

    base:desc "Predictive maintenance insights identifying potential equipment failures before physical manifestation."

    base:desc "Optimized energy consumption scenarios with dynamic load balancing recommendations."
]

]

    base:desc "Optimized scenarios for space utilization, energy efficiency, and operational workflows."
]

]

instance DemandResponseAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated demand response integration for grid interaction and peak shaving."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

]

    base:desc "Automated clash detection between BIM models and real-time operational data during collaborative reviews."
]

    base:desc "Continuous synchronization of digital twin with as-built conditions through automated model updates."

    base:desc "Automated sustainability reports including carbon footprint tracking and energy performance metrics."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The DT system enables **control flows from digital to physical** via validated commands issued by Facility Managers or automated systems. Command types include **adjustments to HVAC systems, lighting controls, or security protocols**, formatted as structured messages compatible with building automation systems. **Validation and authentication mechanisms** are implied to ensure command integrity, though specifics (e.g., cryptographic protocols, access controls) are not detailed. **Feedback systems** provide confirmation of command execution, with bidirectional data exchange ensuring real-time status updates (e.g., acknowledgment of actuator activation). **Conflict resolution** is managed through the DT Framework’s modular architecture, allowing dynamic adjustments without full redeployment. The system emphasizes **holistic building life cycle management**, ensuring commands align with operational requirements derived from enriched BIM models."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, implying sub-second to millisecond-level latency for critical operational monitoring and control. The framework supports **dynamic operational needs** through runtime configuration management, enabling adjustments without full redeployment, which suggests adaptable time scales for different services (e.g., sensor data aggregation, analytics, or user interactions). While exact synchronization frequencies or granularity are not specified, the emphasis on **real-time** implies a requirement for low-latency bidirectional data flows, likely leveraging event-driven or streaming protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) to maintain temporal alignment between physical and digital representations. The system’s modular architecture further allows for service-specific time scales, where high-frequency updates (e.g., IoT sensor streams) may coexist with lower-frequency analytical or reporting services."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **coordinated constellation architecture** centered around a **modular, service-oriented framework** that orchestrates interactions between physical buildings, BIM models, and operational Digital Twins. Key components include:
  - **DT Framework**: Acts as the central hub, facilitating **bidirectional data exchange** and **holistic life cycle management** through standardized extension points.
  - **Role-Based Orchestration**: Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, and DT Engineers contribute to distinct phases (deployment, monitoring, and extension), with the framework ensuring **runtime configuration management** for adaptability.
  - **Component Integration**: Leverages **enriched BIM models** as the foundational digital representation, transformed into operational Digital Twins via the framework’s pipeline. Integration patterns include **modular service plugins** (e.g., analytics, IoT interfaces) and **runtime adjustments** without full redeployment.
  - **Resource Management**: The system prioritizes **scalability** (from individual components to entire infrastructure systems) and **low-overhead maintainability**, with non-functional requirements addressing interoperability and accessibility across heterogeneous systems."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within the BIM environment, enriching static BIM models with **semantic representations** of Building Control System (BCS) components (e.g., functional requirements **F_X** in Table III). This phase establishes a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations.
  2. **Model Transformation**: Leveraging **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**, the framework extracts **structured representations** from enriched BIM models and **automatically generates deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention. This phase ensures seamless transition from static BIM to dynamic DT representations.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, enabling **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts. This phase supports **continuous synchronization** and operational alignment.
  4. **Runtime Operation**: The DT operates with **interactive visualization** for immersive monitoring and control, facilitated by Facility Managers. The **modular architecture** allows integration of custom services and analytics, enabling **evolutionary adaptation** to changing operational needs.
  5. **Extension and Maintenance**: DT Engineers develop and integrate **custom services and plugins** to extend functionality, governed by the framework’s **standardized extension points**. Version control and lifecycle governance are implied through the **modular, extensible architecture**, though explicit methodologies (e.g., versioning protocols) are not detailed in the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **fidelity requirements** by enabling **high-precision device modeling** within BIM, where sensors, actuators, and controllers are explicitly defined to reflect their physical counterparts. **Validation and verification** are implicitly supported through the **automated transformation** of BIM models into deployable DT artifacts, ensuring semantic consistency between physical and digital representations. **Uncertainty sources** may arise from discrepancies between static BIM models and dynamic operational data, though the framework mitigates this via **real-time synchronization** mechanisms. **Error detection** is facilitated through **interactive visualization interfaces**, allowing Facility Managers to cross-validate digital states against physical observations. **Quality assurance** processes are embedded in the **modular architecture**, where custom services (e.g., analytics plugins) can enforce domain-specific validation rules. The framework’s reliance on **structured data extraction** from BIM ensures traceability, while **runtime monitoring** capabilities enable continuous fidelity assessment during DT operation."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT Framework employs a **modular, interoperable network infrastructure** to support heterogeneous system integration. While specific protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) are not named, the architecture prioritizes **scalability** and **interoperability** across components. Network topology is **centralized**, with the DT Framework acting as a hub for bidirectional data exchange between physical assets and digital representations. **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are optimized for real-time synchronization, though exact thresholds are unspecified. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are implied through runtime configuration management, enabling low-overhead updates without redeployment. **Security protocols** for network communication are not explicitly detailed but are inferred to align with non-functional requirements (NF_X) addressing maintainability and accessibility."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT system supports **flexible deployment models**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise hosting**, tailored to operational needs. Computational and storage requirements are designed for **scalability**, accommodating individual components or entire infrastructure systems. The framework’s **modular architecture** allows for standardized extension points, enabling custom service integration without full redeployment. **Scalability mechanisms** leverage runtime configuration management, ensuring low-overhead updates. Deployment models are aligned with the DT life cycle, with Building Control Engineers initiating deployment via BIM tools and Facility Managers interacting with operational DTs. **Availability and reliability** are assured through the framework’s systematic approach to bridging static BIM representations with dynamic AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, and Operations) requirements."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework emphasizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through standardized extension points and **data exchange protocols** compatible with external systems. Key integration aspects include:
  - **BIM Integration**: Enriched BIM models serve as the input for Digital Twin deployment, ensuring alignment with static representations while enabling dynamic operational extensions.
  - **External System Dependencies**: The framework supports integration with **enterprise systems** (e.g., facility management software, IoT platforms) and other Digital Twins via **bidirectional data exchange**, though specific protocols (e.g., IFC, COBie, or proprietary APIs) are not detailed.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: Non-functional requirements explicitly address **cross-system interoperability**, suggesting adherence to industry standards (e.g., OPC UA for IoT, REST/gRPC for service communication) to ensure seamless data flow between physical assets, Digital Twins, and external stakeholders.
  - **Integration Patterns**: The modular architecture allows for **plug-and-play service integration**, enabling DT Engineers to extend capabilities without disrupting existing workflows. Dependencies on external systems are managed through the framework’s **runtime configuration management**, ensuring adaptability to evolving operational needs."
]
